global_context.description: This section exists for human readers only. Agents receive their protocol embedded in each task.
global_context.testing_philosophy: TDD with convex-test + Vitest for logic. Playwright E2E for UI flows. NO React component tests. NO mocks except vi.useFakeTimers() and external network calls.

dev_servers.convex: Convex dev running alongside (npx convex dev)

visual_verification_protocol.description: Every UI component must be visually verified against annotated reference images BEFORE committing. This prevents building components that don't match the user's locked decisions.
steps: 1. After building the component, ensure dev server is running on localhost:3005.
2. Use Claude-in-Chrome to navigate to the page where the component renders.
3. Screenshot the rendered component.
4. Read the reference annotation image listed in the task's visual_reference field.
5. Compare: does the rendered output match the annotation? Check layout, colors, icons, spacing, text.
6. If it does NOT match: fix the component, re-render, re-compare. Do NOT commit until it matches.
7. If it DOES match: proceed to commit.
visual_verification_protocol.gate: Orchestrator must visually verify each component before dispatching the next. Components are built SEQUENTIALLY with verification gates, not in parallel.

task_protocol_template._comment: This exact block is embedded in every task under 'task_protocol'. Agents see it inline — no memory dependency.
steps: 1. READ the task description, acceptance criteria, and files_to_read thoroughly before writing any code.
2. TDD: Write failing tests FIRST (Convex tests for logic, Vitest for utilities). Watch them fail. Then implement code to make them pass.
3. Run ALL verification commands in order: bun run typecheck → bun run build → bun run lint:fix → bun run test → bun run format
4. SMOKE TEST: Verify dev server is still running — curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:3005 must return 200. Check Convex dev has no schema errors.
5. Check acceptance criteria — every single one must pass.
6. If ANY verification or acceptance criteria fails: git stash your changes, report what failed, do NOT commit broken code.
7. Update docs/WIP.md with task completion status.
8. Commit to feat/nutrition branch with descriptive message referencing the task ID (e.g., 'W1-02: Add nutrition goals to profile schema').
9. For UI tasks: Browser-test via Claude-in-Chrome on localhost:3005. Verify the component renders, interacts correctly, and console has no errors.
task_protocol_template.rollback_strategy: If verification fails after 2 attempts to fix: git stash, report the failure with exact error output, and flag the task as BLOCKED in docs/WIP.md. Do NOT push broken code. Do NOT skip failing tests.
task_protocol_template.file_conflict_rule: If another task in your wave modifies the same file, those tasks MUST run sequentially, not in parallel. Check the task's files_to_modify list before starting.

testing_rules.convex_functions: Write convex-test tests in convex/*.test.ts. Use convex-test with real database. No mocks except vi.useFakeTimers().
testing_rules.utility_functions: Write Vitest tests in shared/__tests__/ or src/**/__tests__/. Pure unit tests.
testing_rules.ui_flows: Write Playwright E2E tests in e2e/*.spec.ts. Test real user flows against real backend.
testing_rules.forbidden: NO React component tests. NO render(). NO React Testing Library. NO mock data unless absolutely unavoidable (justify with a comment).

waves.name: Research
waves.description: Read-only analysis tasks that produce decision documents. No code changes. Uses agent-team validation: 3 agents independently research, then cross-validate findings before finalising.

agent_team_protocol.description: Wave 0 uses adversarial validation to prevent research errors propagating into code waves.
steps: 1. THREE agents independently read the same source files and produce their own mapping/analysis.
2. Agents exchange their outputs and identify contradictions, gaps, or assumptions.
3. Agents produce a COMBINED document that resolves contradictions with evidence (file paths, line numbers).
4. The combined document includes a 'Assumptions Requiring Validation' section listing anything that could not be resolved from code alone.
5. Human reviews the Assumptions section before Wave 1 begins.
agent_team_protocol.gate: Wave 1 CANNOT start until Wave 0 outputs are reviewed and the Assumptions section is resolved.

steps: 1. READ every file in files_to_read thoroughly. Do not skim.
2. Produce the deliverable markdown document addressing every point in the description.
3. Include exact file paths and line numbers for every claim.
4. Include an 'Assumptions Requiring Validation' section at the end — list anything you inferred but could not confirm from code alone.
5. Verify: cat the output file and confirm it meets the line count minimum.
6. Commit to feat/nutrition branch with descriptive message.
task_protocol_research.rollback_strategy: Research tasks produce documents only — no rollback needed. If uncertain about a finding, mark it as an assumption, do not guess.

tasks.title: Audit Agent A components vs real data model
tasks.description:
Read all 9 Agent A files and the real domain types. Produce a line-by-line mapping document: for every type, interface, function, and import in Agent A's code, document what the real-app equivalent is (or note it doesn't exist). Key deliverable: a markdown file listing every mock dependency and its replacement.

Specific mappings needed:
- mockData.FoodItem -> real FoodRegistryEntry (shared/foodRegistryData.ts) + ingredientProfiles.nutritionPer100g
- mockData.FoodLogEntry -> real FoodLog (domain.ts) backed by logs table (type='food')
- mockData.SavedMeal -> no real equivalent (defer or cut)
- mockData.FOOD_REGISTRY -> shared/foodRegistry.ts FOOD_REGISTRY (148 entries, no macros/portions)
- mockData.calculateCalories -> must compute from ingredientProfiles.nutritionPer100g.kcal * portion / 100
- mockData.detectMealSlot -> corrected times: breakfast 5-9am, lunch 1-4pm, dinner 8-11pm, else snack
- State.waterIntake/waterGoal -> derive from fluid logs (type='fluid', name='Water') in SyncedLogs
- State.foodLogs -> derive from food logs (type='food') in SyncedLogs
- State.favourites -> no existing storage; needs new field in profiles

CRITICAL: Also audit the ingredientProfiles table to determine which entries already have nutritionPer100g data (kcal, protein, carbs, fat, sugars, fibre). Document coverage: how many of the 148 registry entries have matching ingredientProfiles with nutrition data? This directly feeds W1-01.
files_to_read: [COMPLETED — worktree paths were historical, task output at docs/nutrition-card/data-model-mapping.md]

steps: 1. READ every file in files_to_read thoroughly. Do not skim.
2. Produce the deliverable markdown document addressing every point in the description.

5. Verify: cat the output file and confirm it meets the line count minimum.
6. Commit to feat/nutrition branch with descriptive message.
task_protocol.rollback_strategy: Research tasks produce documents only — no rollback needed. If uncertain about a finding, mark it as an assumption, do not guess.
task_protocol.agent_team: This task runs 3x independently. Outputs are cross-validated before finalising.

tasks.title: Map food pipeline integration points
tasks.description:
Read the existing food parsing pipeline end-to-end and document exactly how the Nutrition card must call it.

Key questions to answer:
1. When user types 'chicken, 200g rice' and hits Log Food, what exact call sequence fires?
2. How does the staging modal's 'Log Food' button work with the pipeline? Pre-populate items with matched canonicals, OR reconstruct rawInput string?
3. How does FoodMatchingModal get triggered for unresolved items? Via useUnresolvedFoodQueue + useUnresolvedFoodToast.
4. What is the mealSlot field format? Lowercase: 'breakfast', 'lunch', 'dinner', 'snack'

Deliverable: markdown doc with exact function signatures, call sequences, and data shapes.

tasks.title: Design portion data schema for food registry
tasks.description:
Design the portion data extension for FoodRegistryEntry.

CRITICAL: The ingredientProfiles table in Convex ALREADY has nutritionPer100g data for many entries. This task MUST audit that table first and design a strategy to PULL existing data from ingredientProfiles rather than hallucinating values.

Required fields per entry:
- defaultPortionG: number
- naturalUnit: string | null ('slice', 'piece', 'tsp', 'cup', null)
- unitWeightG: number | null (weight of one naturalUnit in grams)
- caloriesPer100g, proteinPer100g, carbsPer100g, fatPer100g, sugarsPer100g, fibrePer100g: number

Approach:
1. Query ingredientProfiles table to see which entries already have nutritionPer100g
2. For entries WITH data: map ingredientProfiles.nutritionPer100g fields to the new schema
3. For entries WITHOUT data: document which ones are missing and create a population strategy (NOT agent-hallucinated values — use USDA FoodData Central or the existing ingredientProfiles API)
4. Design a seeding script that pulls from ingredientProfiles at build time

Deliverable: TypeScript interfaces + audit of existing data coverage + seeding strategy.

steps: 1. READ every file in files_to_read thoroughly. Do not skim.
2. Produce the deliverable markdown document addressing every point in the description.
3. Include exact file paths and line numbers for every claim.
4. Include an 'Assumptions Requiring Validation' section at the end — list anything you inferred but could not confirm from code alone.
5. Verify: cat the output file and confirm it meets the line count minimum.
6. Commit to feat/nutrition branch with descriptive message.

acceptance_criteria: TypeScript interfaces defined for portion data extension

Seeding strategy that pulls from ingredientProfiles — NOT hallucinated values
Plan for populating gaps using USDA FoodData Central or equivalent

waves.description: Schema changes, registry extensions, and shared data model work. All schema changes must verify Convex dev picks them up successfully.

tasks.title: Add portion + macro data to food registry (seeded from ingredientProfiles)
tasks.description:
Extend FoodRegistryEntryBase with optional portion/macro fields. Create FOOD_PORTION_DATA map. Populate using data from the existing ingredientProfiles Convex table — NOT by hallucinating values.

New fields (all optional): defaultPortionG, naturalUnit, unitWeightG, caloriesPer100g, proteinPer100g, carbsPer100g, fatPer100g, sugarsPer100g, fibrePer100g.

Approach:
1. Write a seeding script (scripts/seedPortionData.ts) that queries ingredientProfiles and maps nutritionPer100g to the new fields
2. For entries without ingredientProfiles data, flag them — do NOT invent values
3. Create FOOD_PORTION_DATA as a static map seeded from the script output
4. Add utility functions to shared/foodRegistryUtils.ts:
   - getPortionData(canonical)
   - calculateCaloriesForPortion(canonical, portionG)
   - calculateMacrosForPortion(canonical, portionG) -> {protein, carbs, fat, sugars, fibre}

TDD: Write tests for utility functions FIRST. Tests should verify calculations against known values from ingredientProfiles.

Run assertFoodRegistryInvariants + typecheck.

2. TDD: Write failing tests FIRST (Vitest for utility functions: getPortionData, calculateCaloriesForPortion, calculateMacrosForPortion). Watch them fail. Then implement code to make them pass.

tasks.title: Add nutrition goals + favourites to profile schema (combined)
tasks.description:
COMBINED TASK (originally W1-02 + W1-03) to avoid file conflicts — both touch convex/schema.ts, convex/validators.ts, src/hooks/useProfile.ts.

Part A — Nutrition Goals:
Add nutritionGoals field to profiles table in Convex: { dailyCalorieGoal: number, dailyWaterGoalMl: number }. Add mutation to update. Add useNutritionGoals() hook with defaults (1800 kcal, 1000ml).

Part B — Favourites:
Add foodFavourites: string[] to profiles table (array of canonical names). Add mutations: addFoodFavourite, removeFoodFavourite. Add useFoodFavourites() hook.

CONVEX SCHEMA PUSH: After modifying convex/schema.ts, verify Convex dev picks up the schema change. Run 'npx convex dev --once' and check for errors. If schema push fails (e.g., breaking change on existing data), document the error and flag as BLOCKED.

TDD: Write Convex function tests for mutations FIRST (convex-test). Write utility tests for hooks.

steps: 1. READ the task description, acceptance criteria, and files_to_read thoroughly before writing any code.

2. TDD: Write failing Convex tests FIRST (convex/*.test.ts) for: updateNutritionGoals mutation, addFoodFavourite mutation, removeFoodFavourite mutation. Watch them fail. Then implement code to make them pass.
3. Run ALL verification commands in order: bun run typecheck → bun run build → bun run lint:fix → bun run test → bun run format
4. CONVEX SCHEMA PUSH: After modifying schema.ts, run 'npx convex dev --once 2>&1 | tail -20' and verify no errors. If schema push fails, STOP and report.
5. SMOKE TEST: Verify dev server is still running — curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:3005 must return 200.
6. Check acceptance criteria — every single one must pass.
7. If ANY verification or acceptance criteria fails: git stash your changes, report what failed, do NOT commit broken code.

9. Commit to feat/nutrition branch with descriptive message referencing the task ID.

tasks.title: End-to-end integration test: full nutrition flow
tasks.description:
NEW TASK — not in v1 plan. Write a comprehensive Playwright E2E test that verifies the FULL flow works end-to-end:

1. Navigate to Track page
2. Type a food in the search input (e.g., 'white rice')
3. Add to staging via + button
4. Adjust portion in staging modal
5. Click Log Food
6. Verify food appears in TodayLog
7. Verify calorie ring updates
8. Open calorie detail view
9. Verify food appears under correct meal slot
10. Delete the food
11. Verify calorie ring decreases
12. Log water via water modal
13. Verify water progress bar updates

This is the single most important test in the entire plan. If this passes, the feature works.

steps: 1. READ the task description and the entire e2e/nutrition-card.spec.ts file to understand existing test patterns.
2. Write the comprehensive E2E test covering all 13 steps in the description.

5. If ANY test fails: document the exact failure, identify which Wave 3 task produced the bug, flag it.
6. Update docs/WIP.md with task completion status.
7. Commit to feat/nutrition branch with descriptive message referencing the task ID.

tasks.title: Edge cases and error states
tasks.description:
Handle: first-time user (zero-state), no API key (auto-matching unavailable), network error (don't clear staging), long food names (truncate), calorie/water goal of 0 (no division by zero), 20+ staging items (warning), special chars in search (sanitize), rapid +/- (no negatives), empty staging Log Food (disabled).

TDD: Write Vitest tests for edge cases in the reducer (goal=0 division, rapid +/- bounds, special char sanitization). Playwright E2E: test zero-state, empty staging disabled button.

steps: 1. READ the task description and acceptance criteria thoroughly.
2. TDD: Write failing Vitest tests for edge cases in the reducer: goal=0, rapid +/- bounds, special char sanitization, 20+ staging warning. Watch them fail. Then fix.
3. Write Playwright E2E tests: zero-state rendering, empty staging disables Log Food.
4. Implement edge case handling in components.
5. Run ALL verification commands in order: bun run typecheck → bun run build → bun run lint:fix → bun run test → bun run format
6. Run Playwright: bunx playwright test e2e/nutrition-card.spec.ts
7. SMOKE TEST: Verify dev server is still running — curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:3005 must return 200.
8. Check acceptance criteria — every single one must pass.
9. If ANY verification or acceptance criteria fails: git stash your changes, report what failed, do NOT commit broken code.
10. Update docs/WIP.md with task completion status.
11. Commit to feat/nutrition branch with descriptive message referencing the task ID.
12. Browser-test via Claude-in-Chrome on localhost:3005 — test each edge case.
task_protocol.rollback_strategy: If verification fails after 2 attempts to fix: git stash, report the failure with exact error output, and flag the task as BLOCKED in docs/WIP.md.
task_protocol.file_conflict_rule: If another task in your wave modifies the same file, those tasks MUST run sequentially, not in parallel.

testing_rules.tdd_targets: useNutritionStore.test.ts — edge cases: goal=0, rapid +/-, special chars, 20+ staging

acceptance_criteria: No division by zero with goal=0
Empty staging disables Log Food button
Dev server still returns 200 on localhost:3005

tasks.title: Full regression: all tests pass, all E2E pass
tasks.description:
Final verification task. Run the ENTIRE test suite (Vitest + Playwright) and fix any failures. This is the gate before merge.

This task also runs the nutrition-full-flow.spec.ts integration test one final time to confirm end-to-end works after all polish changes.

steps: 1. Run bun run test — ALL unit/Convex tests must pass.
2. Run bunx playwright test — ALL E2E tests must pass.
3. Run bunx playwright test e2e/nutrition-full-flow.spec.ts — the critical integration test must pass.
4. Run bun run typecheck && bun run build && bun run lint:fix — zero errors.
5. SMOKE TEST: curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' http://localhost:3005 must return 200.
6. If ANY failure: fix it, re-run everything, repeat until green.
7. Update docs/WIP.md with final completion status.
8. Update docs/WORK-QUEUE.md marking the entire nutrition card feature as COMPLETE.
9. Commit to feat/nutrition branch: 'W5-04: Full regression — all tests green, feature complete'.
task_protocol.rollback_strategy: If a failure cannot be fixed: document it in docs/WIP.md as a known issue with severity and workaround. Do not mark the feature as complete.
task_protocol.file_conflict_rule: No conflicts — this is a verification-only task.

testing_rules.scope: ALL tests — bun run test + bunx playwright test
testing_rules.forbidden: Do NOT skip any failing tests. Do NOT mark tests as .skip.

acceptance_criteria: bun run test — ALL pass, zero failures
bunx playwright test — ALL pass, zero failures
